Document:
# Definitions
[italic]These definitions are used throughout the rest of Marine Law.[/italic]
## Personnel
[color=#00CBFE][bold]Commanding Officer[/bold][/color] - The official UNMC Commanding Officer of the warship. - Anything with the words [color=#00CBFE][bold]Commanding Officer[/bold][/color] only applies to the current whitelisted Commanding Officer of the warship. These rights can be transferred to any UNMC Officers that are ranked [bold]Major[/bold] or above by the [color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color].
\n
[color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color] - The current [color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color] of an operation. - Transferable to a new [color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color], per the [textlink="Commander Succession" link="RMCCommandChain"].
- If the current [color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color] is rendered [bold]permanently[/bold] incapable of command, the next in the [textlink="Commander Succession" link="RMCCommandChain"] becomes the new [color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color].
[italic]Examples include but are not limited to:[/italic]
- The [color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color] being relieved of duty due to [bold]arrest[/bold] or [bold]mutiny[/bold]. - The [color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color] becoming [bold]unrevivable[/bold].
Ensure when assigning a new [color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color], that this is announced. Include the new [color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color]'s name and the reason for them being assigned as the new [color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color], to avoid confusion.
\n
[color=#90EE90][bold]Duty Officer[/bold][/color] - The ranking officer who is placed in command of the operation, in the absence of the [color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color]. - A [color=#90EE90][bold]Duty Officer[/bold][/color] should be assigned any time the [color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color] is no longer in the CIC during periods where the main combat force is active in an operation. - The [color=#90EE90][bold]Duty Officer[/bold][/color] should not leave the CIC unless there is a danger to themselves, or the [color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color] is rendered [bold]temporarily[/bold] incapable of shipside command. - If the [color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color] is rendered incapable of shipside command [bold]temporarily[/bold], the [color=#90EE90][bold]Duty Officer[/bold][/color] inherits all authority of the [color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color] until they return, including leaving CIC to handle related matters, such as appeals.
[italic]Examples of temporary cases include but are not limited to:[/italic]
- Prolonged communications outage, where the [color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color] can not be contacted. - The [color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color] deploying in the field, to lead the combat force for an extended period. (In this case, the deployed [color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color] still has priority in orders that are issued.)
\n
[color=#8B0000][bold]Chief MP[/bold][/color] - The Chief of Military Police, assigned to the warship. - Only transferable to the [bold]Warden[/bold] by the [color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color], if another [color=#ff000d][bold]MP[/bold][/color] is appointed to be the acting [bold]Warden[/bold] in their place. - An acting [bold]Warden[/bold] cannot be the acting [color=#8B0000][bold]Chief MP[/bold][/color] - A deputized person may not be the acting [bold]Warden[/bold].
\n
[color=#ff000d][bold]MP[/bold][/color] - Military Police, including validly deputized personnel, who deal with Marine Law.
\n
[bold]Officer[/bold] - A person who is a Commissioned Officer. These rights cannot be transferred. - A person who is a Warrant Officer. These rights cannot be transferred.
\n
[bold]Department Heads[/bold] - A person who is responsible for an entire department, which includes the [bold]Chief Engineer[/bold], [bold]Chief Medical Officer[/bold], [bold]Auxiliary Support Officer[/bold], [bold]Logistics Officer[/bold], [color=#8B0000][bold]Chief MP[/bold][/color].
\n
[bold]CIC Personnel[/bold] - Standard personnel whose main duties are within CIC. This includes the [color=#00CBFE][bold]Commanding Officer[/bold][/color], [bold]Executive Officer[/bold], and [bold]Staff Officers[/bold].
\n
[bold]Flight Crew[/bold] - Designated Pilots and Dropship Crew Chiefs.
## Orders
[bold]Lawful Orders[/bold] - Personnel are required to follow all lawful orders from their superiors and are not required to follow unlawful orders. - If personnel are refusing to follow an order, they must state why they believe it would break Marine Law, making it an unlawful order. - Valid NJPs are not unlawful orders in the case of neglected duties.
\n
## Equipment
[bold]Standard Issue Equipment[/bold]
- [bold]Standard Issue[/bold] equipment is defined as any military uniform, armor, weapon, or accessory personnel find in their crew vendors, as well as anything they emerge from hypersleep with or anything normally available from requisitions.
\n
[bold]Riot equipment[/bold] - Flashbangs. - Teargas. - Riot shotguns with beanbag rounds. - Riot shields and armor. - Riot grenade launchers. - Rubber bullets.
\n
[bold]Recorder[/bold] - Any device, capable of recording dialogue and reproducing it in any form at a later time. - SOP on the use of [bold]Recorders[/bold] by [color=#ff000d][bold]MP[/bold][/color] can be found [textlink="in department non-modifiable SOP." link="RMCMarineSOPNONModDepartment"]
\n
[bold]Listening Device[/bold] - Any covert device, capable of transmitting dialogue and/or recording and reproducing it in any form at a later time. - SOP on the use of [bold]Listening Devices[/bold] by [color=#ff000d][bold]MP[/bold][/color] can be found [textlink="in department non-modifiable SOP." link="RMCMarineSOPNONModDepartment"]
\n
## Areas
Further definitions and requirements for these areas can be found in Non-Modifiable Command SOP.
[bold]Secure Areas[/bold]
- These areas dictate where certain shipside personnel are allowed to be given permission to work safely. - The [bold]FOB[/bold] and [bold]Field Hospital[/bold] are typical [bold]Secure Areas[/bold].
\n
[bold]FOB[/bold] - The Forward Operating Base, usually used for staging combat personnel and storing excess supplies. - Designated by the [color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color], or otherwise usually the [bold]Primary Landing Zone[/bold].
\n
[bold]Primary Landing Zone[/bold]
- Selected for the entire duration of the operation by the [color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color]. - The primary drop-off and evacuation point.
\n
[bold]Area of Operations (AO)[/bold] - The area of operations is the broad location where personnel are being deployed to conduct an operation.
\n
[bold]Field Hospital[/bold] - A [bold]Secure Area[/bold] designated by the [bold]Chief Medical Officer[/bold] or [color=#008000][bold]Commander[/bold][/color]. - It is the only area aside from the [bold]FOB[/bold] may receive permission to doctors and nurses to move to.
\n
## Alerts
[bold]Alert Levels[/bold] - Code [bold][color=lightgreen]Green[/color][/bold]: All clear. - Code [bold][color=lightblue]Blue[/color][/bold]: Potentially hostile activity on board. - Code [bold][color=red]Red[/color][/bold]: There is an immediate threat to the warship or it's crew. Examples of usage include; AI Core Security issue, biohazard containment breach, general quarters. Distress beacon, evacuation, and usage of sentry systems authorized. - Code [bold][color=black]Delta[/color][/bold]: Immediately applies if the warship hull has a major breach by intruders. An order of evacuation, self-destruction, or hold of the warship will be made at this time.
\n
## High Ranks and Provost
[bold]High Ranking Officers[/bold] - UNMC Officers ranked [bold]Major[/bold] or above. - They may have some additional provisions, which can be found [textlink="in command non-modifiable SOP." link="RMCMarineSOPNONModCommand"] - Officers designated as "High Command" may perform [bold]Battlefield Executions[/bold].
\n
[color=#ffa500][bold]Provost Marshal Office[/bold][/color] - Hold significant weight on how Marine Law is handled in their operational areas. - In the event that Marine Law is being mishandled aboard a UNMC warship or within the [bold]AO[/bold] of a UNMC warship, [color=#ffa500][bold]Provost Marshal Office[/bold][/color] may see fit to dispatch personnel. - Members of the [color=#ffa500][bold]Provost Marshal Office[/bold][/color] obey only their direct superiors and are not beholden to Marine command or officers. - Provost [bold]Marshals[/bold] are not required to follow Marine Law, and may perform [bold]Battlefield Executions[/bold].\nThey may also at their discretion alter the Standard Operating Procedure, including Non-Modifiable sections. - Provost [bold]Inspectors[/bold] and above are the final word on Marine Law in their [bold]AO[/bold]. - Provost [bold]Advisors[/bold] do not hold any direct authority themselves, however they are expected to ensure Marine Law is correctly handled.
